opener.dateline:
Legation of the French Republic in the United States,
Washington, September 27, 1892. (Received September 29.)

div.p: In a letter dated 27th of August last, Mr. Alvey A. Adee communicated to me the copy of a telegram that you were kind enough to address to Mr. Patenôtre on board of the Bretagne to inform him of the dismissal of the policeman, who, on the 30th of May last, in the town of Jeannette, Pa., tore the French flags placed by one of our countrymen in the window of his house.

I am instructed by the minister of foreign affairs to acquaint you that the French Government appreciates the satisfaction accorded it in this case by the Federal Government, at the request of Mr. Patenôtre.
